parsed_review.summary: CP00-519 (AI Governance Core P02 Workflow Slice, RP17.P02.M04.S16-S25, risk A, 10 units) is a clean descriptor-only pack. Pack identity, range, and deliverable distribution {implementation:1, failure_recovery:2, test:4, fixture:1, hermes_evidence:1, claude_review:1} are consistent across registry.js binding, manifest, review-baseline, closeout-pack-plan.json, the contract current_pack/cp519_requirements/p02_workflow_slice_descriptor projection, and the historical_pack_bindings tail. All 10 M04 rows are descriptor_only with runtime_execution:false, customer_safe_errors_only:true, raw_payload_included:false; freezeCp519Result applies the full no-write/no-runtime/no-real-data flag set and grep confirms no file writes, network, subprocess, or env access anywhere in packages/ai-governance/src. The Claude authority boundary holds everywhere (claude_is_final_approval:false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust:false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true), and Hermes is used only for deterministic evidence/receipt descriptors (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false). validate-rp17-ai-governance-core-contract.mjs deep-equals the contract against code-generated descriptors/requirements/attestations, and model.test.js adds three CP519 tests (coverage, descriptor-only rows, evidence/handoff boundaries). command-evidence records implementation/plan/repo validations at exit 0 with production_ready:false and no_real_data:true; the post-review gated commands are correctly deferred. The pack does not over-claim: production_ready and runtime_ready are false, and only the descriptor-verification candidate (coverage.valid && slice.valid) is asserted, which is fully supported by the evidence available at review time. Review-hardening preconditions pass (auth_preflight exit 0 with valid JSON, prompt 2189B under limit, tools limited to Read/Grep/Glob, forbidden_review_evidence encodes the CP178+ rejects). No P0/P1/P2 findings; one informational P3. Unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room, workbook/docx) are correctly preserved and excluded from pack scope.

findings.title: Post-review hardening artifacts pending by design; descriptor-verified is not a runtime/enterprise-trust claim
findings.details: At this review (hardened sequence step 3 of 9, read_only_claude_run_raw_capture), the post-review artifacts listed in manifest.required_pack_artifacts/evidence_refs - claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json - are not yet present, and pack_level_claude_review.status is 'pending' with empty valid_review_receipts. This is expected and correct: those artifacts are produced by the downstream steps (normalize -> receipt_validate -> finding_adjudication -> construction_inspection) that consume this review, so their absence is not a defect. manifest.status is 'implemented_pending_review', production_ready:false, runtime_ready:false. The only positive claim made now is the descriptor-level production_ready_candidate (coverage.valid && slice.valid), which is fully supported by registry/service/validators/contract deep-equal sync and model.test.js. enterprise trust and any runtime opening (CP00-520+) still require human final approval per the authority boundary.
findings.recommendation: Proceed through the remaining hardening sequence and re-run npm run closeout-pack:validate plus git diff --check --cached before commit. Continue treating the descriptor-verified flag as descriptor-scope only; do not let any downstream step promote Claude to final approver or claim enterprise trust from local validation.
findings.post_review_disposition: Accepted as the expected pending-review state; non-blocking. Recorded to reinforce that descriptor verification does not equal runtime or enterprise readiness.
